description_long: This is a vintage brass telescope mounted on a sturdy tripod stand, reminiscent of 19th-century astronomical instruments. The main body is a long, cylindrical tube with a metallic sheen, supported by a decorative, turned central column. The tripod base features three elegantly curved legs that provide stability, each ending in a flat foot. The telescope is affixed to the stand with a circular adjustment mechanism, allowing for rotation and angling. The metallic surfaces show a polished, slightly reflective finish, with some patina suggesting age. The overall construction is robust, with visible screws and adjustment knobs for fine-tuning the telescope's orientation.
description: A vintage brass telescope with a polished cylindrical tube mounted on an ornate, three-legged tripod stand. The stand features curved legs and adjustment mechanisms for angling the telescope.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular opening of the telescope tube and the symmetrical arrangement of the tripod legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the brass tube and the decorative central column of the tripod.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the eyepiece end of the telescope and the tripod's leg spread.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the adjustment mechanism and the full length of the tube.
